layer1_micro.label: body_check
layer1_micro.text: Rest day. You slept ten hours and felt it in warmups.

layer1_micro.label: body_check
layer1_micro.text: Everything felt sharp in warmups. Hard to say why. You take it and move on.

layer1_micro.label: body_check
layer1_micro.text: You were tired from the minute you showed up. You did the work anyway.

layer1_micro.label: body_check
layer1_micro.text: Hit the wall and worked through it. That's the adaptation you're after.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Coach made a point of flagging your effort today. Doesn't say it often.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Coach adjusted your lead-leg angle. Clearance margin improved within two reps.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Coach pulled film -- your jump next to a reference athlete. Now you see it.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Coach ran a tactical scenario -- pace, kick, position. You handled it.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Three extra curve reps. Something is clicking in the lean.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
layer1_micro.text: One cue on entry angle. You adjusted, and the distance improved noticeably.

layer1_micro.label: bye_week_rest
text: No meet this week. You use the extra time to train well and rest better.
Bye week -- a chance to dial things in without the pressure of a result.
No race to prepare for. You put in solid work and let the body recover.

layer1_micro.label: micro_energy
text: Cold morning practice. Your joints take extra time to come around.
Hard to get warm in this weather. You fight through the first 30 minutes.
Early season cold -- the kind that makes every rep feel like twice the work.

layer1_micro.label: micro_energy
text: Your legs are light and responsive. Good week to push.
Full tank heading into practice. Every step felt loaded.
You've been sleeping well. It shows.

layer1_micro.label: micro_energy
text: A genuine recovery day -- pool work, foam rolling, eight hours of sleep.
Coach kept it light. You come out the other side feeling recharged.
You resist the urge to go hard and it pays off. Body feels ready.

layer1_micro.label: micro_energy
text: Nothing clicked today. The body just wasn't cooperating.
You got through practice, but it felt like dragging weight the whole time.
Off day. You do the work anyway and try not to read too much into it.

layer1_micro.label: micro_energy
text: You're running on fumes but you grind through it. The body remembers.
Heavy legs, slow warmup. You get through the session on will alone.
Everything costs more right now. You do it anyway.

layer1_micro.label: micro_focus
text: You take a few minutes before practice to just breathe. Clears the noise.
A quiet moment between warmup sets. You find your focus and hold it.
Something about today -- the air, the light -- put you fully in the present.

layer1_micro.label: micro_focus
text: Championship season. Scenarios run through your head all day.
The closer it gets, the sharper the focus. Everything becomes preparation.
You know what's coming. You've been building toward it for months.

layer1_micro.label: micro_form
text: Something clicked in warmups. The angle was right and the timing followed.
Coach adjusted one cue and suddenly the mechanics felt natural.
A small tweak in your setup and the whole movement pattern cleaned up.

layer1_micro.label: micro_form
text: New drill from coach today. Awkward at first, but you feel what it targets.
New warmup pattern. Takes a while to click, but something starts to shift.
You spend extra time on a drill coach has been emphasizing. The reps add up.

layer1_micro.label: micro_training
text: Another long run in the books. The base keeps building.
Easy miles this morning. Nothing dramatic, just the accumulation of work.
You hit every mark on the long run. The aerobic base is there.

layer1_micro.label: micro_training
text: Finding the rhythm early in the season. The mechanics aren't sharp yet, but they're coming.
First week back in full swing. You're rusty, but you remember.
Early season momentum. A little rough, but you're building toward something.

layer1_micro.label: micro_training
text: You hit a set that felt almost effortless -- pure power output.
The starts were explosive and the follow-through felt locked in.
Best power numbers of the week. The legs had something extra today.

layer1_micro.label: micro_training
text: Every interval clicked to the right split. Something about today just worked.
The pace felt automatic. You barely had to think about it.
Effortless rhythm. Coach barely said a word -- nothing to correct.

layer1_micro.label: micro_training
text: Everything feels aligned this week. You're in a groove.
Mid-season form -- the best kind. Fit, sharp, and not yet worn down.
The work from earlier in the year is showing up now. You can feel it in the numbers.

layer1_micro.label: micro_training
text: Every rep clicked today. The session ran clean from warmup to cooldown.
One of those days where nothing felt like work. You came out of it sharper.
Clean technique, good pace, no wasted effort. Everything landed.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: You stayed after practice to work on something nagging. Now it's better.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: Extra reps on trail-leg mechanics. Won't show in a time, but it's there.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: The pop off the board felt loaded. You've worked weeks for that feeling.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: An easy run turned into one of those rare days. You could have gone forever.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: Your breathing was controlled through the whole session. Didn't tighten up once.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: The last rep was the best one. That's the stuff you can take into a race.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: Stride out of the blocks felt cleaner today. Hard to explain, but it was there.

layer1_micro.label: practice_moment
layer1_micro.text: Eight of ten throws landed in the same sector. That's not luck.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Hit a lifetime PR in the weight room today. Small number, but it counts.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: The drive phase into the first hurdle was sharp. You attacked it clean.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Spacing felt natural for a full set today. Rare this early in the week.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Your approach counted out perfectly today. Every step where it should be.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Landing mechanics work -- repetitive, but you came out of it more controlled.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Twelve miles at a steady clip. The kind of work that compounds.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Five AM run in the dark. The first mile was rough. The rest wasn't.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Every tempo interval hit exactly where it should. Didn't check the watch once.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Your block clearance felt clean today. Low and driving, the way it should be.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Heavy squats today. You hit your top set and didn't grind it.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Forty throws on release angle today. It's starting to feel automatic.

layer1_micro.label: training_feedback
layer1_micro.text: Strong power cleans before throwing. You felt it in the hip drive.

layer1_micro.label: logistics
layer1_micro.text: Team bus breaks down. Warmup time gets cut short.

layer1_micro.label: schedule
layer1_micro.text: Practice gets canceled for weather. You switch to recovery work.

layer1_micro.label: teammate_success
layer1_micro.text: A teammate breaks the school record. The whole group feels sharper.

layer1_micro.label: media
layer1_micro.text: Local paper mentions your last meet. Expectations tick upward.

layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
text: You stayed after practice for block starts. Ten more, coach said. It was closer to twenty.
Extra block work in the empty stadium after everyone left. The last few starts felt like they belonged to a faster athlete.
Coach crouched behind the blocks and watched your first step over and over. By the end, the track came up to meet you quicker.

layer1_micro.label: training_adjust
text: The week's mileage went up and the junk miles came out. Every run had a job this week.
Coach added a mile to the morning runs and cut the shakeout nobody was recovering from. Your legs noticed both.
More volume this week, but cleaner -- long runs with purpose, no filler. You finished Friday tired in the right way.

layer1_micro.label: technique_adjust
text: Your throwing coach moved your release a half-beat earlier. The implement stopped wobbling and started carrying.
Release timing work all week. By Thursday the flight looked flat and clean, the way it does on film.
One adjustment at the point of release, and the throw sounded different coming off your hand. Better different.

layer1_micro.label: team_culture
text: The captain pulled everyone in before reps. Thirty seconds, no speech, just a reminder of what the group is building. It worked.
A quick huddle before the main set. The captain said one true thing and broke it down. The whole session ran hotter after.
Before reps, the captain called the group up. You've heard longer talks that did less.

layer1_micro.label: training_overload
text: Two sessions in the same day gets the work done, but your body keeps a little of the bill.
You stacked a double this week. Useful, but the recovery cost is not imaginary.
A two-session day leaves a little residue in the legs. Nothing dramatic. Just there.

layer1_micro.label: training_overload
text: Three sessions in a week like this buys progress, but you can feel the recovery lag behind.
You pushed through a triple. Good work, but the body is slower to bounce back now.
A heavy training stack this week leaves you feeling just a step less fresh.

layer1_micro.label: training_overload
text: Four sessions in one week is a statement. It also takes something real out of you.
You emptied a lot into training this week. The cost shows up in recovery and general resilience.
A full overload block leaves your body feeling tougher in some ways and more fragile in others.

layer1_micro.label: competitive_slide
text: Three rough regular-season meets in the same year and you can feel the doubt trying to creep in.
Another bad league result and the self-talk gets louder. You have to work to shut it down.
A run of weak finishes starts to linger in your head longer than you want it to.

layer1_micro.label: competitive_slide
text: You are stacking training weeks, but the meets tell a harsher story. It is getting harder to ignore.
Practice says one thing. Race day says another. After two flat results in a row, that gap starts to sting.
You know the body is improving, but coasting through races is starting to cost you mentally.

layer1_micro.label: competitive_slide
text: Half the season is gone without a regular-season podium. You can feel your edge dulling.
No podiums yet, and the sport starts to feel like something happening to you instead of something you control.
Race rhythm is hard to fake. Without a real result to build on, the season starts to wear on you.

layer1_micro.label: competitive_slide
text: You keep piling on hard work, but the results are not there yet. The grind starts to feel heavier.
Three heavy weeks with little payoff. Your body keeps showing up, but the wear is real.
The volume is there. The outcomes are not. That kind of mismatch takes something out of you.

layer1_micro.headline: RIVALRY TURNS AGAINST YOU
layer1_micro.label: rival_escalation
text: The same rival keeps getting the better of you, and people are starting to notice.
You lost to the same rival again. It stops feeling random after the third time.
One matchup is turning into a story, and right now you are on the wrong side of it.

layer1_micro.headline: COACH INTERVENTION
layer1_micro.label: coach_feedback
text: Coach pulls you aside after another rough result. The message is direct, and it lands hard.
Midseason check-in. Coach does not sugarcoat it: the results are not good enough, and everyone knows it.
Coach intervention comes this week. The feedback is useful, but it still feels like a blow.

layer1_micro.label: coach_tip
text: Your throwing coach spends extra time on your release angle. Small tweak, real payoff.
Release point work today. You feel the difference on the first full throw after.
Coach keys in on your wrist position at release. Something to think about in competition.

layer1_micro.label: coach_tip
text: Extra block start work. Your first step has been sharper since.
Coach keeps you on the track after practice to drill reaction time. It is working.
Block refinement today. You can feel the difference in your first ten meters.

layer1_micro.label: coach_tip
text: Your hurdle coach adjusts your trail leg mechanics. Your clearance tightens noticeably.
Lead leg drills with the hurdles flat. Your coach wants more precision. You deliver.
Step pattern work between hurdles. The rhythm is cleaner now.

layer1_micro.label: coach_tip
text: Your jump coach works your penultimate step. Your takeoff has more pop now.
Approach mechanics today. The small adjustments to your last two steps show up in height and distance.
Coach films your takeoff and plays it back. What you see surprises you. So does the improvement after.

layer1_micro.label: coach_tip
text: Your coach walks you through your splits from the last meet. The pacing tells a story. You listen.
Tempo run today with specific split targets. You hit them all. That is the mental side of distance.
Your coach talks about the first 200 meters killing careers. You file that away.

layer2_world.label: meet_buzz
layer2_world.text: Top athletes scratched from the upcoming meet. The field thinned overnight.

layer2_world.label: meet_buzz
layer2_world.text: This week's meet is at your home track. You know every inch of it.

layer2_world.label: meet_buzz
layer2_world.text: A college scout added next week's meet to their schedule. Source is reliable.

layer2_world.label: meet_buzz
layer2_world.text: The posted entries for next week's meet are out. It's a loaded field.

layer2_world.label: meet_buzz
layer2_world.text: Wind forecast on meet day. You start thinking through what that changes.

layer2_world.label: rival_update
layer2_world.text: A competitor from across the district posted a standout performance at an invitational this weekend. The region is paying attention.

layer2_world.label: rival_update
layer2_world.text: Rumor on the circuit: a top regional athlete is dealing with a hamstring.

layer2_world.label: rival_update
layer2_world.text: A PR from the athlete ranked ahead of you. A narrow margin, but it counts. You note it and move on.

layer2_world.label: rival_update
layer2_world.text: Your main competition scratched from the last invitational. Reason unknown.

layer2_world.label: team_update
layer2_world.text: Coach called a team meeting. Message was clear: standards are rising.

layer2_world.label: team_update
layer2_world.text: Long bus ride to a scrimmage and back. Not ideal recovery, but the reps counted.

layer2_world.label: teammate_success
layer2_world.text: Your teammate PR'd in practice today. Energy in the room is high.

layer2_world.label: team_update
layer2_world.text: A teammate is grinding through a rough stretch. You keep your focus and say nothing.

layer2_world.label: world_buzz
text: Championship is already getting talked about. The field looks deep.
Hallway chatter about who shows up at championships. Your name comes up.
The whole conference is tracking results as championship season approaches.

layer2_world.label: world_buzz
text: A few college programs have been tracking regional results. Word travels.
A D1 assistant was at last week's invitational. Watching who, nobody knows.
Recruiting season is running in the background. Results are being watched.

layer2_world.label: world_circuit
text: A competitor went down with an injury at a recent meet. Sobering reminder.
Tough news on the circuit: a solid athlete from a rival school is out for the season.
One of the region's top runners is injured. You process it quietly.

layer2_world.label: world_circuit
text: National results are out. The marks at the top of the country are circulating.
Someone in the group chat posts the national rankings. The numbers are serious.
You look at the national leaderboard. Then at your own marks.

layer2_world.label: world_coach
text: Coach pulls you aside after practice. 'Your form is cleaning up. Keep it up.'
Coach texts after reviewing splits. Short message: 'This is the work.'
A quiet word from coach after the session. 'I can see it all coming together.'

layer2_world.label: world_coach
text: Coach pulls film from last year. The difference in your mechanics is visible to everyone.
Video review today. Watching yourself from two seasons ago is strange.
Coach plays your year-one form next to last week. Progress.

layer2_world.label: world_context
layer2_world.text: College track season is underway. Marks are circulating. You keep a mental note.

layer2_world.label: world_context
layer2_world.text: Unseasonably hot this week. Practice moved to early morning to beat the heat.

layer2_world.label: world_context
layer2_world.text: The local paper ran a preview of the district championship. Your name is in it.

layer2_world.label: world_context
layer2_world.text: Rain cancelled outdoor practice Thursday. Coach adjusted the week on the fly.

layer2_world.label: world_context
layer2_world.text: State rankings updated. You moved up two spots.

layer2_world.label: bye_week_rest
text: No meet this week. You use the downtime to genuinely recover.
Bye week and you actually took it seriously. You feel the difference.
The extra rest was the right call. Your body needed this window.

layer2_world.label: world_rival
text: Word travels fast: {rival_name} posted a mark this week that will turn heads.
The results from a nearby invitational are in. {rival_name} had a big day.
Someone screenshots the results and sends them around. {rival_name} ran well.

layer2_world.label: world_rival
text: Buzz on the circuit: {rival_name} has been dealing with an off-stretch lately.
Heard through the grapevine that {rival_name} missed a meet this weekend.
Reports from other coaches suggest {rival_name} isn't quite right physically.

layer2_world.label: world_rival
text: Coach pulls up film of {rival_name}. One habit stands out immediately.
You spend ten minutes in the film room with {rival_name}'s last meet on loop.
A small pattern shows up on video: {rival_name} gives away more than they think.

layer2_world.label: rival_update
text: People keep circling the same matchup this week: you and {rival_name}.
The talk around the meet is not subtle. Everyone expects you and {rival_name} to be near each other.
Even teammates are asking about {rival_name} now. The matchup has a life of its own.

layer2_world.label: world_rival
text: No meet this week, but {rival_name} is still in your head during training.
Bye week. Somehow that gives you more time to think about {rival_name}, not less.
With no meet on the schedule, the rivalry with {rival_name} gets quieter but not smaller.

layer2_world.label: rival_update
text: You can feel it in the way people talk: you've made {rival_name} a real measuring stick.
Two good results against {rival_name}, and now the rivalry feels official.
The matchup with {rival_name} is turning into a reference point for your whole season.

layer2_world.label: world_team
text: The team feels flat this week. Mid-season fatigue hitting everyone at once.
Practices are quiet. Nobody's dragging their feet, but the spark isn't there.
Something about this stretch always wears people down. This year is no different.

layer2_world.label: world_team
text: Big energy in the building today. Home meet, and people actually showed up.
The crowd is loud for a regular-season meet. It picks everyone up.
Home crowd today. Teammates are pumped and the energy carries into warmups.

layer2_world.label: teammate_success
text: A teammate crushed a PR in practice today. The energy in the room shifted immediately.
Good news from the team: two others hit season bests in this morning's time trial.
The training group is peaking. A teammate's big PR in practice lifts everyone.

layer2_world.label: program_shift
text: Coach rewrote the week on Sunday night: fewer sessions, sharper ones. You could feel the difference by Wednesday.
The training plan changed this week -- less grinding, more precision. Practice ended earlier and meant more.
Coach cut two junk workouts and doubled down on the ones that matter. The week felt lighter and heavier at the same time.

layer2_world.label: rival_transfer
layer2_world.text: Word is a strong rival transferred into your district this month.

layer2_world.label: facility
text: New plates and a rebuilt rack showed up in the weight room Monday. Small thing. The sessions run different now.
The school finally replaced the equipment everyone had learned to work around. A week of training without improvising.
Fresh equipment in the training room this week. Nobody made a speech about it, but every session ran cleaner.

layer2_world.label: team_culture
text: The captain started calling out missed reps this week -- their own first. The standard moved and everyone felt it.
New rule from the captain, enforced by example: nobody coasts the last rep. The week sharpened around it.
The captain ran every cooldown with the slowest group this week. Nobody talked about it. Everybody noticed.

layer2_world.label: schedule
text: Two away trips and a test week landed on the same seven days. Sleep lost the negotiation.
The bus, the homework, the make-up quiz -- the week never left you a full night. You trained through it anyway.
Everything scheduled itself into the same week. Recovery happened in the gaps, when it happened at all.

layer2_world.label: training_adjust
text: A full week on drive phase and posture. Boring reps, until Friday, when the first thirty meters came easy.
Coach filmed your drive phase Monday and again Thursday. The second video looked like the athlete you're trying to become.
Mechanics block this week: wall drills, sled pulls, posture cues on a loop. Your shadow started looking right.

layer2_world.label: training_adjust
text: Threshold week. Every run lived right at the edge of comfortable, and by Friday the edge had moved.
A week of tempo work at the red line without crossing it. You finished sessions tired but never broken.
Coach parked the whole week at threshold pace. Ugly on Tuesday, manageable on Thursday, almost smooth by Saturday.

layer2_world.label: facility
text: The throwing circle got resurfaced over the weekend. Your feet stopped negotiating and started working.
New surface in the ring this week. The first few throws felt strange. The rest felt planted.
They finally fixed the worn spot in the circle where every spin used to slip. Full turns, full trust.

layer3_major.label: breakthrough_signal
layer3_major.text: Running on empty for weeks. Something clicked today. You came out of practice feeling like yourself again.

layer3_major.label: breakthrough_signal
layer3_major.text: Everything in your body is telling you this is the week. You can't explain it, so you don't try.

layer3_major.label: major_breakthrough
text: Something snapped into place during warmups. Stride, breathing, arm swing -- it all synced at once.
An ordinary practice turned into something else. You leave with the feeling that a ceiling just moved.
You can't explain it, but everything worked today. Coach noticed. You noticed. Something shifted.

layer3_major.label: major_conditions
layer3_major.headline: Perfect Conditions
text: Race day forecast: no wind, perfect temperature. Every athlete in the field will be going for it.
Conditions are ideal today. The kind that show up a few times a season at most.
Zero wind, cool and dry. Coach says it plainly: 'Everyone's going to run fast today.'

layer3_major.label: major_injury
layer3_major.headline: Injury Scare
text: You feel a sharp pull midway through a drill. You stop, breathe, and walk it off. Nothing serious -- but a warning.
A moment of alarm during intervals. Something twinges and your heart rate spikes. You back off and it clears.
You tweak something coming out of a rep. It eases in a few minutes, but the session is over.

layer3_major.label: major_media
layer3_major.headline: Press Coverage
text: A regional prep blog ran a feature on you by name. Strange to see yourself in print.
Someone screenshots a prep article and sends it to the team chat. There you are, in print.
A regional outlet covered the district standings and highlighted your progression. It's strange to read.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
layer3_major.text: The local college program invited you to a joint practice. You went rep-for-rep with older athletes for two full hours.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
layer3_major.text: An athlete you respect spent an hour working with you after practice. You left with two things to fix and a clearer picture of where you're headed.

layer3_major.label: rival_transfer
layer3_major.headline: Rival Transfer News
text: Stunning news: your most dangerous rival just transferred into your conference.
You find out at the same time as everyone else -- your biggest competition is now in your section.
The transfer news hits the group chat hard. You read it twice to make sure.

layer3_major.label: major_scout
layer3_major.headline: Scout in the Stands
text: Word reaches you before warmups: a Division I assistant made the trip to watch regional prospects today.
Coach pulls you aside before the meet. 'There's a college scout in the stands. Just run your race.'
You spot the unfamiliar face on the infield with a clipboard. Someone's watching today.

layer3_major.label: major_setback
layer3_major.text: You spent two days this week fighting something off. You trained through it, but it cost you.

layer3_major.label: major_staff
layer3_major.headline: Coaching Change
text: Mid-season shakeup: your assistant coach took a job at another program. New voice in the building.
The coaching staff changed this week. You adjust to a different set of cues and expectations.
Unexpected news at practice: a key coach is gone. The program doesn't pause -- but you notice the gap.

layer3_major.label: rival_escalation
text: You end up in the same warm-up area as {rival_name} at an invitational. Neither of you acknowledges it.
{rival_name} takes the lane next to yours in warmups and acts like it means nothing.
You and {rival_name} go through the same drills ten yards apart. Nobody has to say anything.

layer3_major.label: major_media
text: A local write-up frames the meet around one storyline: you against {rival_name}.
By meet day, the matchup with {rival_name} is bigger than just your event group.
People are talking about you and {rival_name} like the result has been scheduled in advance.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
text: Coach sees something on film against {rival_name} and rebuilds part of the plan around it.
A scouting session on {rival_name} gives you one adjustment you trust immediately.
You finally spot the detail in {rival_name}'s approach that had been bothering you all season.

layer3_major.label: major_rival
text: Senior year changes the feel of it. Every week with {rival_name} now has an edge to it.
You realize there are only so many more times you'll see {rival_name} on a heat sheet.
The rivalry with {rival_name} feels heavier this year because both of you know it ends soon.

layer3_major.label: scout_visit
layer3_major.text: A scout from the state university was spotted at warmups. Word travels fast. You try not to think about it.

layer3_major.label: major_breakthrough
layer3_major.text: Your time trial was so fast they had to double-check the stopwatches.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
layer3_major.text: The coaching staff spent extra time with you after practice working on technique.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
layer3_major.text: Coach brought the team's top athletes in to see a sports psychologist.

layer3_major.label: major_setback
layer3_major.text: A virus swept through the locker room. You tried to train through it. You should not have trained through it.

layer3_major.label: major_conditions
layer3_major.text: The stands filled up for this one. Every athlete on the team has people in the crowd tonight.

layer3_major.label: major_setback
layer3_major.text: Sleepless nights wondering if you are really good enough to win at states.

layer3_major.label: major_media
layer3_major.text: Rumors of scouts and scholarships swirl through the program. Your name keeps coming up.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
layer3_major.text: The hardest practice of the season ended with everyone still standing. Nobody quit. You carried something off the track that wasn't there in the morning.

layer3_major.label: major_opportunity
layer3_major.text: The bar moves faster than it did a month ago, and your practice shirts fit tighter through the shoulders. Your body is doing some of the work for you this year.
